subject: Re: Removal of outer membrane signal in M/Relion
body:
Hi Doulin,

Sorry for the delay here: this ended up in spam.


>    1.
>
>    Is there a way to downweight the signal from the membrane when I am
>    processing in M, or does the membrane signal have to be dealt with in the
>    RELION step?
>
> The only way to affect alignments in M would be to attenuate the membrane
signal in the reference volume - you could try using OccuPy for this (
https://occupy.readthedocs.io/en/latest/gallery/membrane/)

>
>    1.
>
>    These complexes are not very abundant (1-4 per tomogram), and I was
>    wondering if doing a multi-species refinement with ribosomes would help
>    with combating the membrane alignment.
>
> You are currently in a regime where low frequency membrane features are
dominating your alignment. Improving your tilt series alignments may help
move you into a regime where higher frequency particle info can start to
contribute to alignment but this is contingent on the membrane not
dominating your alignments at your current resolution.

Cheers,

Alister


On Sat, 19 Oct 2024 at 22:38, Doulin Shepherd <doulin.shepherd@gmail.com>
wrote:

> Hi Warp Team,
>
> I just have a few questions about my data processing steps.
>
> For some background, the complex that I am working on spans the
> periplasmic space and is anchored to the inner and outer membranes. I can
> get a reasonable average using EMAN2, but when I use Dynamo and/or RELION
> 5, it seems that the outer membrane is dominating the alignment and density
> of the reconstruction.
>
> So far, I have used a Fourier space mask to reduce some of the membrane
> signal in the final reconstruction (RELION 5). When I apply the mask, the
> features I see are what I expect, but they are washed out by the membrane
> signal. I assume the alignments would be better if the membrane signal were
> reduced.
>
> With that being said, I have two main questions:
>
>    1.
>
>    Is there a way to downweight the signal from the membrane when I am
>    processing in M, or does the membrane signal have to be dealt with in the
>    RELION step?
>    2.
>
>    These complexes are not very abundant (1-4 per tomogram), and I was
>    wondering if doing a multi-species refinement with ribosomes would help
>    with combating the membrane alignment.
>
> Thanks in advance,
> Doulin
>
> --

> .
>
